title: EEOC — Filing a Charge of Discrimination
description: Scrape of the EEOC charge-filing guidance page covering discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex (incl. pregnancy, transgender status, sexual orientation), national origin, age (40+), disability, and genetic information under Title VII, the ADA, the ADEA, and GINA. Explains intake via the EEOC Public Portal, the pre-suit requirement, dual-filing with state FEPAs such as MCAD, and the distinct federal-sector process.
notes: DEADLINE: 180 days from the discriminatory act, or 300 days where a state/local FEPA (such as MCAD) also has jurisdiction — Massachusetts qualifies, so 300 days for most MA workers. Federal-agency charges use a different process. File ~7.3 KB.
